title: Try It Yourself - Pulling Latest Change on Branch

content.problemStatement:
**Imagine this scenario:**

You are working on `branch-third`, but your teammate updated `branch-first` directly on GitHub (they added a new file).
Your local system still has the old version of `branch-first`, so you need to **pull the latest changes** from GitHub to update your local branch.

---

**Your Task**: Pull the Latest Changes on `branch-first`
Follow these steps:

**Step 1: Switch to the branch you want to update** \
Move to branch-first on your local machine:
```sss
git checkout branch-first
```
**Step 2: Pull the latest updates from GitHub** \
Bring all new changes from GitHub into your local branch:
```sss
git pull origin branch-first
```
**Step 3: Check your git history** \
Your local branch-first is now updated with the latest changes your teammate made on GitHub. 

**You'll See :**
